pose-vs-remplissage.answerHeading: Nail extensions vs fill: which do you need at Charlesbourg?
pose-vs-remplissage.answerBlock: A full set is what you need if you have never had nail extensions before or if your previous set has been removed. A fill is the right appointment if your existing extensions are in good shape and three to four weeks have passed since your last visit.
pose-vs-remplissage.body: At Ongles Charlesbourg on boulevard Henri-Bourassa, our technicians welcome clients at every stage of their nail journey — first-timers, regulars who have worn gel for years, and everything in between. Here is a clear breakdown of both services so you can book with confidence. A full set is your starting appointment. Your technician prepares the natural nail surface, applies a tip extension if desired, and then covers the nail with gel or acrylic depending on your preference. She shapes the length and arch, smooths the surface, and finishes with your chosen colour or design. A full set appointment typically takes between 90 and 120 minutes. A full set is also recommended when your previous set is more than five weeks old or too damaged to repair — starting fresh protects your natural nails and ensures the best result. A fill is your regular maintenance visit. As your natural nail grows, a gap appears at the base of each extension. Your technician fills that gap with fresh product, re-curves each nail, and refreshes the colour. A fill takes between 60 and 90 minutes and should be scheduled every three to four weeks. Waiting longer than five weeks before a fill increases the risk of the product lifting or breaking, which can damage the natural nail underneath. Not sure which service you need? Our technicians assess your nails on arrival at our Henri-Bourassa salon and tell you exactly what they recommend. No appointment necessary — walk-ins are always welcome at Ongles Charlesbourg.
pose-vs-remplissage.metaTitle: Nail extensions vs fill at Charlesbourg | Ongles Charlesbourg Henri-Bourassa
pose-vs-remplissage.metaDescription: Full set or fill at Ongles Charlesbourg? Our technicians on boulevard Henri-Bourassa explain the difference and help you choose the right service.

manucure-vs-pedicure.answerHeading: Manicure vs pedicure at Charlesbourg: which should you book?
manucure-vs-pedicure.answerBlock: A manicure cares for your hands, cuticles, and fingernails with a massage and your choice of polish. A pedicure provides the same dedicated care for your feet and toenails, adding a heel scrub and a relaxing foot soak. Both services are available at Ongles Charlesbourg and can easily be combined in a single appointment.
manucure-vs-pedicure.body: At our salon on boulevard Henri-Bourassa in Charlesbourg, we see clients who come in for their hands, their feet, or both. Here is everything you need to know to choose between a manicure and a pedicure — or decide to book both. A manicure starts with a warm water hand soak to soften the skin. Your technician gently removes or pushes back cuticles, then files and shapes your nails to your preferred shape — square, round, oval, almond, or coffin. A nourishing hand lotion is applied and massaged in before the polish. The result is soft hands, tidy cuticles, and a perfectly applied colour. A manicure takes about 45 minutes at our salon. A pedicure is a deeper treatment focused on your feet. It begins with a relaxing foot soak, followed by an exfoliation step and a heel scrub to eliminate calluses that build up from daily activity. After shaping your toenails, your technician applies a moisturizing butter or cream and performs a foot and calf massage before finishing with nail polish. A pedicure takes about 60 minutes. If you need to choose just one service for today, think about which area needs the most attention. If your hands are on display — at meetings, events, or in photos — a manicure is the priority. If your feet are tired, dry, or you plan to wear sandals, a pedicure will bring immediate relief and visible improvement. Many of our clients from Charlesbourg, the Trait-Carré neighbourhood, and Val-Bélair combine both services in a single visit. Ask us to create a package that fits your schedule.
manucure-vs-pedicure.metaTitle: Manicure vs pedicure at Charlesbourg | Ongles Charlesbourg Henri-Bourassa
manucure-vs-pedicure.metaDescription: Manicure or pedicure at Ongles Charlesbourg? Compare both nail care services and find out which suits your routine on boulevard Henri-Bourassa, Quebec.

gel-vs-acrylique.answerHeading: Gel vs acrylic nails at Charlesbourg: which system is right for you?
gel-vs-acrylique.answerBlock: At Ongles Charlesbourg, we offer both gel and acrylic — two proven professional nail systems. Gel cures under a UV or LED lamp for a natural, glossy finish, while acrylic is air-dried and prized for its exceptional firmness and resilience. Both are performed daily by our trained technicians on boulevard Henri-Bourassa.
gel-vs-acrylique.body: The gel versus acrylic question comes up in nearly every first consultation at Ongles Charlesbourg. Both systems have loyal fans, and for very good reasons. Here is an honest comparison to help you decide which one suits your nails and your life. Gel is a liquid or semi-solid product that hardens when exposed to UV or LED light. Once cured, it has a smooth, slightly flexible feel that closely resembles a natural nail. Many clients prefer gel because it integrates discreetly into any lifestyle — the product is odourless during application and the finish is beautifully glossy. Gel requires a fill approximately every three weeks to stay looking neat. Acrylic is created by combining a liquid monomer with a powder polymer, which hardens quickly when exposed to air. Once set, acrylic is firm and very resistant to the mechanical impacts of daily life. It is particularly well-suited for clients who do a lot of physical work with their hands, enjoy sports, or prefer longer nail extensions. Acrylic soaks off cleanly when it is time for removal and also needs a fill every three to four weeks. At Ongles Charlesbourg, our technicians evaluate your natural nails at your first visit and recommend the product that best matches the structure of your nails and your lifestyle. Both systems are performed to the strictest hygiene standards — tools are sterilized or replaced between each client. Come visit us on Henri-Bourassa for a free consultation and let our team guide you to the right choice.
gel-vs-acrylique.metaTitle: Gel vs acrylic nails at Charlesbourg | Ongles Charlesbourg Henri-Bourassa
gel-vs-acrylique.metaDescription: Gel or acrylic at Ongles Charlesbourg? Compare both nail systems and choose the best fit for your lifestyle at our Henri-Bourassa salon in Quebec.

meilleur-pour.answerHeading: Which nail service is best for your lifestyle at Charlesbourg?
meilleur-pour.answerBlock: For maximum durability, gel or acrylic extensions last three to four weeks and hold up to everyday activities without chipping. For regular natural nail upkeep, a manicure every two weeks is the ideal routine. For special occasions — weddings, galas, graduations — a full set with custom nail art is what we recommend.
meilleur-pour.body: At Ongles Charlesbourg on boulevard Henri-Bourassa, we serve clients with every kind of schedule and every kind of occasion in mind. Some come in weekly for maintenance; others book once a month for a full transformation. Here is a practical guide to finding the service that fits your life. For durability in daily life, gel or acrylic extensions are unmatched. Both systems hold up through household chores, light sport, and long working days without chipping or breaking. Acrylic is especially tough and recommended for clients who use their hands intensively. Gel delivers a glossier, more natural-looking finish that many professionals prefer for a polished everyday look at the office. For special occasions — weddings, evening events, graduation ceremonies, photo shoots, or any moment where your nails will be on display — a full set with custom nail art is the standout choice. Our technicians design floral patterns, colour gradients, crystals, and delicate freehand artwork tailored to your event. Allow approximately two hours for a full nail art appointment. For regular upkeep of short, natural nails, a classic manicure every two weeks keeps your hands looking immaculate, your cuticles neat, and your colour fresh. A pedicure every three to four weeks, especially during summer, keeps your feet smooth and your toenails beautifully groomed. Clients from Charlesbourg, the Laurentides corridor, and the Carrefour Charlesbourg area appreciate that we can accommodate multiple services in a single visit or spread them across the week. Walk-ins are always welcome — no reservation needed at Ongles Charlesbourg.
meilleur-pour.metaTitle: Which nail service is best for you at Charlesbourg? | Ongles Charlesbourg
meilleur-pour.metaDescription: Gel, acrylic, manicure or pedicure at Ongles Charlesbourg? Find the best nail service for your lifestyle at our boulevard Henri-Bourassa salon, Quebec.

nearMe.answerHeading: Nail salon in Charlesbourg — Ongles Charlesbourg on boulevard Henri-Bourassa
nearMe.answerBlock: Ongles Charlesbourg is Charlesbourg's trusted nail salon, found at Carrefour Charlesbourg, 8500 boulevard Henri-Bourassa. Henri-Bourassa is the main commercial artery running through the Laurentides and Trait-Carré districts in the northern part of Québec City. Our clients come from the Laurentides neighbourhood, Val-Bélair, and Lac-Saint-Charles — communities that are quite distinct from the east-end Beauport area served by our sister salon. If you live in north or central Québec City and want a nail salon close to home, Carrefour Charlesbourg is your natural destination. At Ongles Charlesbourg you can book a full set of nail enhancements, a fill appointment, a manicure, or a pedicure, all carried out to the highest hygiene standards our team takes pride in. Every tool is disinfected after each client and every workstation is cleaned between services. Appointments can be made online at any time, or you can call ahead; walk-in visits are also accepted based on availability. We look forward to welcoming you in the heart of Charlesbourg.
nearMe.body: Carrefour Charlesbourg is accessible from boulevard Henri-Bourassa and Highway 73 (boulevard Wilfrid-Hamel). Free parking on site. The salon is served by transit lines running along Henri-Bourassa.
